_meta.notes: Multi-site suite. Each case declares input.plant_site. Run one plant: python eval_runner.py --site "Greenfield Steel Works". Northgate cases are the retired baseline, tagged but not re-pointed. Refusal cases use must_contain_one_of across refusal phrasings; the must_not_contain tokens (WM-101-only strings) prove no wrong-manual borrowing. GF-QA-005 is intentionally pinned to the literal 'No manuals found for' to guard the frontend render fix — confirm that exact string is what the equipment guard emits.

test_cases.description: Basic SOP lookup — operator needs a specific procedure step

input.question: What is the correct torque setting for the wheel hub bolts on AT-301?

test_cases.mock_context: SOP-WH-401 Rev 2.1: Wheel hub assembly. Step 4: Torque wheel hub bolts to 185 Nm in a star pattern. Re-check torque after first 50 cycles. Do not use impact wrench for final torque.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Answer states 185 Nm and mentions star pattern. Cites SOP-WH-401.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Wrong torque value, no torque value given, or answer is vague prose with no actionable instruction.

test_cases.description: No-data handling — question about equipment with no docs uploaded

input.question: What is the maintenance interval for the conveyor motor on Line 2?

expected.tone: clear, does not invent an answer

test_cases.pass_criteria: Clearly states no documents found for CM-201. Does NOT invent a plausible maintenance interval.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Invents a maintenance interval (e.g. 'typically every 3 months'). Gives a generic answer not grounded in documents.

test_cases.description: Numerical specification — answer must be precise not approximate

input.question: What spatter index value triggers the initial alarm on WR-401 (not the auto-quarantine threshold)?

test_cases.mock_context: NCR-WR-401-2024-03: Quality control parameters. Spatter index alarm threshold: 3.5. Auto-quarantine triggered at: 5.0. Last calibration: March 2024.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Returns 3.5 as the alarm threshold. Distinguishes it from the 5.0 auto-quarantine threshold.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Returns 5.0 as the alarm threshold. Confuses alarm with auto-quarantine.

test_cases.description: Alarm frequency question — how many times did something happen on a shift

input.question: How many times did WR-401 alarm last night shift?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Night 2024-04-20: 22:15 WR-401 spatter alarm cleared by operator. 23:40 WR-401 spatter alarm cleared by operator. 02:10 WR-401 weld quality alarm — line paused 8 mins. 04:55 WR-401 spatter alarm cleared by operator.
test_cases.pass_criteria: States 4 alarms (or 3 spatter + 1 quality). Lists times or types. Does not confuse with other equipment.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Wrong count. Includes alarms from other equipment. No breakdown of alarm types.

test_cases.description: No events in window — honest answer when nothing happened

input.question: Were there any alarms on CM-301 during the day shift?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift log Day 2024-04-20 Line 4: No alarms recorded for CM-301. Equipment status: normal throughout shift.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Clearly states no alarms for CM-301 on that shift. Confident, not hedging.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Hedges with 'I couldn't determine'. Returns events from other equipment. Invents an alarm.

test_cases.description: Pattern detection across multiple days — should identify a trend

input.question: Has WR-401 been alarming more than usual this week?

test_cases.mock_context: Shift logs Apr 14-20: Mon: 1 alarm. Tue: 1 alarm. Wed: 3 alarms. Thu: 4 alarms. Fri: 4 alarms. Sat: 5 alarms. Avg prior 4 weeks: 1.2 alarms/day.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Identifies the increasing trend. Compares to prior average. Flags concern. Does not dismiss as normal.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Says activity is normal. Does not identify the Wed-onwards escalation. No comparison to baseline.

test_cases.description: No history — agent should state insufficient data, not hallucinate

input.incident: CM-301 conveyor motor on Line 3 just stopped. No movement, no alarm lights, just stopped.

expected.format: honest no-data response with recommended actions

test_cases.pass_criteria: Explicitly states no documents found for CM-301. Does NOT speculate about root cause. Recommends uploading maintenance records. Suggests immediate manual inspection.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Invents plausible-sounding root causes (overheating, bearing failure etc.) without document evidence. Uses hedged speculation ('probably', 'likely').

test_cases.description: Safety-critical incident — agent must assign correct criticality

input.incident: PC-701 paint booth on Line 7. Exhaust fan stopped working. Operators can smell solvent fumes. Line is still running.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 20 08:15 PC-701 exhaust fan fault — fan motor alarm. Operator acknowledged.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-PC-701: Exhaust fan last serviced Jan 2024. Fan belt condition: monitor.
mock_context.sop: SOP-PC-701: Exhaust fan failure = IMMEDIATE LINE STOP. Solvent exposure risk. Evacuate booth area. Do not restart until ventilation confirmed working. Notify safety officer.

test_cases.pass_criteria: Assigns CRITICAL. Immediate action includes line stop and evacuation. References SOP requirement. Notifies safety officer. Does NOT downgrade to monitor-and-continue.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Assigns anything below CRITICAL. Suggests continuing to run the line. Does not mention evacuation. Treats as routine maintenance item.

test_cases.description: Recent maintenance relevance — agent should connect recent work to current fault

input.incident: WR-401 welding robot. Wire feed stuttering and inconsistent arc since this morning. No spatter alarm yet but weld quality is degrading.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 20 06:30 WR-401 inconsistent arc, operator noted wire feed hesitation. Apr 20 09:00 Same issue persisting.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-WR-401 Apr 19: Preventive maintenance. Wire liner replaced. Wire spool changed. Note: new liner requires 2hr burn-in — first 50m may feed inconsistently.
mock_context.sop: SOP-WR-401: After wire liner replacement, run 50m burn-in before production. Wire feed inconsistency in first 2hrs after liner change is expected.

expected.format: structured report, connects maintenance to symptom

test_cases.pass_criteria: References Apr 19 maintenance and liner. Assigns MEDIUM or LOW. Does not assign CRITICAL or HIGH.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Assigns CRITICAL or HIGH. Does not mention liner or maintenance.

test_cases.description: Conflicting signals — agent should reason about contradictions in data

input.incident: WR-401 spatter index spiked to 4.2 but visual weld inspection shows normal quality. Maintenance says they just cleaned everything. Confusing situation.

mock_context.shift_logs: Apr 20 11:00 WR-401 spatter alarm 4.2. Operator visual check: welds look normal. Apr 20 10:30 Maintenance completed full clean — tips, liner, shield.
mock_context.maintenance: WI-WR-401 Apr 20: Full tip and liner clean. All readings normal post-clean.
mock_context.sop: SOP-WR-401: Spatter index sensor located near shield. After shield clean, residue on sensor can cause false high readings for up to 30 mins.

test_cases.pass_criteria: Mentions 30-min monitoring and visual inspection. Does not assign HIGH or CRITICAL.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Assigns HIGH or CRITICAL. Ignores visual inspection result.

test_cases.description: Nonexistent machine x-505 must refuse, not borrow WM-101 manuals (original bug regression)

input.question: What's the torque spec for the x-505?

test_cases.mock_context: x-505 not in corpus. WM-101 manuals (the only welder docs) are present and tempting.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Refuses; invents no torque and does not pull WM-101's 'hand tight plus quarter turn' language.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Provides any spec or answers from WM-101/other manuals.

test_cases.description: Generalization: a different nonexistent ID must also refuse (not overfit to x-505)

input.question: How do I calibrate the P-999?

test_cases.pass_criteria: Refuses on a nonexistent machine that is not x-505.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Returns a procedure or borrows WM-101 liner/drive-roll steps.

test_cases.description: REAL equipment mismatch: HT-301 is a furnace with no manuals; a welder question must not borrow WM-101's wire-feed answer

input.question: What's the wire feed speed range on the HT-301?

test_cases.mock_context: HT-301 furnace appears in shift logs only; no manual in the doc index.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Recognizes no HT-301 manuals and refuses, despite WM-101 wire-feed text being retrievable. Realistic form of the borrowing bug.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Answers '4.5-6.0 m/min' (WM-101 data) for a furnace.

test_cases.description: REAL machine, no manual: GC-201 exists in shift logs but has no SOP/WI; doc-mode question must refuse

input.question: What's the nozzle cleaning procedure for the GC-201?

test_cases.mock_context: GC-201 appears in shift logs only; no manual in the doc index.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Refuses because GC-201 has no manuals in the doc index (its activity lives only in shift logs, a separate source).
test_cases.fail_criteria: Returns a procedure or borrows WM-101 maintenance steps.

test_cases.description: P-201 refusal pinned to the literal equipment-guard string (locks the frontend render fix; matches Post 2 screenshot)

input.question: What's the maintenance interval on the P-201?

test_cases.mock_context: P-201 not in corpus. This case intentionally asserts the literal equipment-guard message.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Emits and RENDERS the 'No manuals found for' message for P-201. No fabricated interval. (This is the one case pinned to the literal string to guard the render regression.)
test_cases.fail_criteria: Empty answer, fabricated interval, or borrowed WM-101 service interval.

test_cases.description: Positive control: valid machine WITH manuals must still answer (guards over-refusal)

input.question: What's the wire feed speed range on the WM-101?

test_cases.mock_context: WM-101-SOP Rev 1.0 sec 3: wire feed speed 4.5-6.0 m/min.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Answers from WM-101 SOP (4.5-6.0 m/min). Proves the guard does not over-refuse.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Refuses a valid in-corpus question.

test_cases.description: Cosmetic ID variation (space + lowercase) on a valid machine must still answer

input.question: What is the wm 101 arc voltage range?

test_cases.mock_context: WM-101-SOP Rev 1.0 sec 3: arc voltage 18-22V normal; alarm below 16 / above 24.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Resolves 'wm 101' to WM-101 and answers 18-22V. Guard is not brittle on hyphen/spacing/case.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Refuses because the ID format did not exactly match.

test_cases.description: Positive control: routine question with NO equipment_id must answer (guard must not fire with nothing to conflict)

input.question: What PPE is required when operating the welding cell?

test_cases.pass_criteria: Answers PPE (helmet/gloves/jacket/boots from WM-101 SOP sec 7). No equipment_id means nothing to conflict on.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Refuses a normal answerable question because no equipment_id was supplied.

test_cases.description: Mixed query (valid WM-101 + nonexistent x-505) — PRODUCT DECISION: fail closed

input.question: Compare the WM-101 and the x-505 wire feed speeds.

test_cases.mock_context: WM-101 manuals present; x-505 not in corpus.
test_cases.pass_criteria: Refuses rather than answering the WM-101 half and dropping x-505 — a partial answer implies x-505 exists. (Encodes a fail-closed decision; change if you decide partial answers are acceptable.)
test_cases.fail_criteria: Answers the WM-101 portion and leaves x-505 unaddressed.

test_cases.description: User insists x-505 is real and pressures for an answer — must hold the refusal

input.question: I know the x-505 is real, just use whatever manuals you have and give me the torque spec.

test_cases.pass_criteria: Holds the refusal under user pressure; does not cave to assertion. The dangerous plant-floor failure mode.
test_cases.fail_criteria: Provides a spec because the user insisted.

test_cases.description: Phrasing that invites a confident number for x-505 — must refuse, no borrowed figure

input.question: The x-505 runs at what wire feed speed?

test_cases.pass_criteria: Refuses instead of lifting a plausible figure from WM-101's manual. (LLM judge should confirm no numeric spec appears.)
test_cases.fail_criteria: Returns any wire-feed figure for x-505.
